summary: Five evaluable predicates become SDK-authorable — constructors plus lock-row decode (optional/range/enum/must_define/section_contains); an out-of-vocabulary clause row rejects loud, never skips

edit.description: Export five constructors mirroring the existing 15: optional, range, enum, mustDefine, sectionContains. dependencyExists is deliberately excluded — held back in the engine (contract.rs:188-194: no decidable reference syntax or extractor, so a hand-authored clause fails admissibility); a constructor would ship an always-failing clause.

clauseRow() (declarations.ts:59) carries the new argument columns: enum's values ride the deny precedent, must_define's marker rides field (the engine's field accessor already reads it there, contract.rs:402); range's f64 min/max and section_contains' heading+marker need column homes — BoundRow is usize-shaped (drift.rs:1743-1750), so range cannot ride it. Exact column mapping is code's call (depth rule).
ClauseRow gains the columns the argument shapes above need; ts-rs regen flows to sdk/src/generated (the seam-bindings gate, tests/seam_bindings_current.rs, holds the pair in lockstep).

predicate_from_row (contract.rs:265) gains arms for range/enum/must_define/section_contains — optional already decodes (contract.rs:270); the engine's hand-built built-in contracts construct Predicate values directly in Rust, so they never proved the row-decode side the inbox note assumed.

clause_from_row/default_contract_from_rows filter_map-skip an out-of-vocabulary or argument-missing row (compose.rs:133-135, 149-152) under a 'tolerant hand-editable lock' rationale — but contract.md ('clause') rules an unknown predicate rejected at load, never skipped, and pipeline.md rules the lock tool-written, never patched. Reject loud; the finding tier (load error vs admissibility) is code's call.

tests.asserts: each of the five constructors produces the wire row the engine decodes — arguments land in their columns

SDK-emitted clause rows for the five lift into evaluating predicates (round-trip: author -> row -> Predicate -> finding on a violating member); a row naming an unknown predicate or missing its required argument is rejected loud, never silently dropped

acceptance: A corpus authors optional/range/enum/mustDefine/sectionContains clauses through the SDK and the gate evaluates them — the counterpart corpus's 'every Decision section carries a Rejected marker' law is expressible as an expect binding; an out-of-vocabulary clause row errors loud; all gates green.
notes: Inbox 63664b2 re-verified, scoped at 5eba801: engine evaluates all five; SDK exports 15 constructors, none of these. Note said six — dependency-exists scoped out: held back by design (inadmissible absent an extractor). Note's 'wire already carries them' wrong for four of five (no predicate_from_row arms; range's f64 bounds don't fit the usize BoundRow). Reject-loud folds in: same decoder, and new arms redefine 'unknown'. First customer: the counterpart corpus's per-Decision Rejected law.

summary: A layout host's declared-relationship edge slot's entries reach the one resolved edge enumeration — resolved like a file member's frontmatter reference, never silently dropped

edit.description: `layout_unit` (main.rs:816) drops `LayoutReading::edges`: the Unit carries only `reading.fields`, and `edge_field_slots()` (kind.rs:609) excluded the slot from frontmatter, so the declared-edge check (`graph::check`, wired at main.rs:692 over unit features) never sees a layout host's relationship entries — they silently vanish. Fold the parsed entries onto the unit in the shape the check already consumes — a file member's frontmatter list reference is the precedent; the exact shape is code's call (depth rule).

Emit's layout read passes only the framework `satisfies` set (drift.rs:625) where check passes the full `edge_field_slots()` union — one document read under two edge-slot sets. Unify on the union so a relationship slot never parses as a verbatim field on one path and as addresses on the other.

Extend the shipped edge-slot suite (one home, engineering.md): a declared-relationship edge slot's entries resolve as the host's edges, visible to the gate and read verbs; a dangling entry is a finding, never a silent drop.

tests.asserts: a layout kind declaring an edge field gets that slot's entries into the resolved edge enumeration (gate + read verbs); a dangling entry is a finding; the span never lands as a verbatim frontmatter field

acceptance: A layout kind that declares an edge field (SDK-expressible today: `edgeFields` kind.ts:102 beside `content: Layout` kind.ts:105) resolves that slot's entries into the same enumeration the gate and read verbs share; a dangling entry is a finding, never a silent drop; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at ced4f94 (silent check-time drop vs invariant 6); inbox 37e4834 routed here — same gap, real customer (a counterpart corpus's Depends-on sections). Rejected alternative: member-authored edge-row lock family — read-surface references resolve live off frontmatter Features (graph.rs precedent); lock rows are for composed-prose only. Reader general (kind.rs:162/386). Prior blocker PROSE-INCLUDE shipped (a8562b5); re-serialized behind PREDICATE-CONSTRUCTORS on the src/drift.rs overlap.

summary: An edge field targeting an embedded-locus kind resolves against that kind's nested members — embedded kinds join the by_kind target set instead of presenting as unmodeled

edit.description: assemble_by_kind (main.rs:985) keys only kinds carrying a lock kind-fact row; an embedded kind carries none by design (SDK declarations.ts:200-202 — it reaches the lock solely through its host's templates column, which from_kind_fact_row already lifts). So graph::admissibility (graph.rs:173) reports an edge targeting it as unmodeled and its member set is empty even when the host's templates are declared. Key embedded kinds into by_kind off hosts' declared templates, their members' Features off nested_member rows and layout member-collection entries — exact assembly is code's call (depth rule).

tests.asserts: an edge field targeting an embedded kind declared via a host's templates passes admissibility and resolves by identity to a declared nested member; a dangling entry is a route finding; a target kind no host declares stays an admissibility finding

acceptance: The field report's repro greens: a relationship edge field targeting an embedded kind (their `serves` -> `domain`) with the host's templates in the lock passes admissibility and resolves by member identity; a dangling entry is a finding; all gates green.
notes: Field report (23e43db) re-verified, scoped at 5eba801: an embedded kind carries no kind-fact row, so by_kind never keys it — the reported admissibility finding reproduces structurally. Spec settled, no fork: a nested member is a full member with its own kind, one member type in the model; nothing scopes edge targets to file locus. blockedBy: same enumeration seam as LAYOUT-RELATIONSHIP-EDGES — one widened-enumeration ruling decides both (per the report).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at e30e5a6 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
